title: Tones with no mark
pedagogy: Tone rules (2/4): unmarked syllables — mid+live=mid, mid+dead=low, high+live=rising, high+dead=low, low+live=mid, low+dead-short=high, low+dead-long=falling.

exercises.title: No mark ≠ no tone
exercises.thaiExample: กา ขา มา
body: A bare syllable still has a tone. The class of the initial consonant plus live/dead decides which.
LIVE: mid class → mid tone (กา), high class → rising (ขา), low class → mid (มา).
DEAD: mid and high class → low tone (กะ). Low class splits: high tone on a short vowel (คะ), falling on a long one (มาก).

exercises.title: Three anchor words
body: กา (mid + live) = mid tone, "crow". ขา (high + live) = rising, "leg". มา (low + live) = mid, "to come".
Keep those three as tuning forks — every unmarked syllable maps onto one row of the table.
Now drill until the table is reflex.

exercises.promptNote: ka — ก is mid class; live
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Mid class + live = mid tone. กา means "crow".

exercises.promptNote: kha — ข is high class; live
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: High class + live = rising tone. ขา means "leg".

exercises.promptNote: ma — ม is low class; live
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Low class + live = mid tone — unmarked live low behaves like mid.

exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: High class + live = rising tone. ขอ means "to ask for".

exercises.promptNote: nok — low class; dead, short vowel
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Low class + dead + short vowel = high tone. นก means "bird".

exercises.promptNote: mak — low class; dead, long vowel
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Low class + dead + LONG vowel = falling tone. มาก means "very".

exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Mid class + live = mid tone. จาน means "plate".

exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: High class + live = rising tone. สี means "color".

exercises.promptNote: kha — low class; dead, short vowel
exercises.question: Which tone?
exercises.explanation: Low class + dead + short = high tone. คะ is the high-tone polite particle.
